author: rādhāvallabha dāsa
verses:
āre mora āre mora gaurāṅga gosāñī
dīne dayā tomā vinā kare keha nāi

ei ta’ brahmāṇḍa⋅mājhe jata reṇu⋅prāya
ke gaṇibe pāpa mora gaṇana nā jāya

manuṣya durlabha janma nā hoibe āra
tomā nā bhajiyā kainu bhā̐ḍera ācāra

hena prabhu nā bhajinu ki gati āmāra
āpanāra mukhe dilāma jvalanta aṅgāra

kena vā āchaye prāṇa ki sukha lāgiyā
vallabhadāsiyā kena nā jāya mariyā
en_translation:
Oh my, oh my, my Lord Gaurāṅga! No one else shows mercy to the fallen like You.

In this universe, there are as many dust particles as there are sins of mine—they are countless.

This rare human birth will not come again. Without worshipping You, I have acted only like a fool.

Having not worshipped such a Lord, what will be my fate? I have effectively put burning coals into my own mouth.

Why do I still live? For what happiness? Why does Vallabha Dāsa not simply die?
